iraola.summary: High-intensity, vertical 'controlled chaos'. Base 4-2-3-1 (flexes to 4-3-3). Signature is a hybrid press — zonal shape that springs man-to-man traps — plus an aggressive high line, immediate counterpress and direct, wide attacking. Among the most physically demanding systems in Europe (Bournemouth led the PL for distance covered and lowest PPDA).
needs: Ball-playing libero: calm vs the press, brave off his line, accurate short and long.
Front-foot defenders who step into midfield and recover quickly behind a high line; line-breaking passers.
Tireless two-way engines that stretch build-up then attack the byline; second-ball hunters.
Do-everything engine room: beat the press, win second balls, counterpress instantly. Athleticism non-negotiable.
Pace, 1v1 directness and aggressive off-ball running/pressing.
Thrive in pockets and on second balls; striker runs in behind, holds up and floods the box.

squad.name: Alisson Becker
squad.fitNote: Ideal spare-man libero — elite distribution short and long, calm under the press.

squad.name: Giorgi Mamardashvili
squad.fitNote: Modern ball-playing keeper; strong competition/cover.

squad.name: Armin Pécsi
squad.fitNote: Young third-choice; one for the future.

squad.name: Virgil van Dijk (c)
squad.fitNote: Fits the line-breaking 'Senesi' role; only question is pace behind a high line.

squad.name: Jérémy Jacquet
squad.fitNote: New £55m signing from Rennes; athletic front-foot CB. Recovering from March shoulder surgery.
squad.injury: Shoulder surgery (Mar 2026) — expected fit for pre-season

squad.name: Joe Gomez
squad.fitNote: Versatile and athletic but injury record makes him depth, not a cornerstone.

squad.name: Giovanni Leoni
squad.fitNote: Composed, progressive — future depth; unproven at this intensity.

squad.name: Jeremie Frimpong
squad.fitNote: Blistering pace and run-timing made for the attacking-FB role; balance/injury caveat.

wc.note: Left out of NED squad (injury record)

squad.name: Conor Bradley
squad.fitNote: Sound two-way RB with pace and work-rate; good for balance.

squad.name: Calvin Ramsay
squad.fitNote: Fringe RB depth.

squad.name: Milos Kerkez
squad.fitNote: Arguably the best fit — thrived under Iraola at Bournemouth as a marauding LB.

squad.name: Ryan Gravenberch
squad.fitNote: The 'Tyler Adams' of the pivot — driving carries and physicality Iraola loves.

squad.name: Wataru Endo
squad.fitNote: Pure screener; lacks the two-way range Iraola's pivot demands.

squad.name: Stefan Bajcetic
squad.fitNote: Right profile but injuries leave him as long-term depth.

squad.name: Dominik Szoboszlai
squad.fitNote: A 'perfect fit' — receives between lines, threads passes, presses intensely.

squad.name: Alexis Mac Allister
squad.fitNote: Technically superb but physical output over 90 mins is questioned for this pivot.

wc.note: Opener vs Algeria 17 Jun (not yet played)

squad.name: Curtis Jones
squad.fitNote: Energetic, press-resistant two-way profile fits the 8 role.

squad.name: Trey Nyoni
squad.fitNote: Highly rated teenager; squad depth/future.

squad.name: Florian Wirtz
squad.fitNote: Earmarked for the hybrid No.10 role; chaos-and-pockets system seen as the platform to unlock him.

squad.name: Alexander Isak
squad.fitNote: A 'dream No.9' — runs in behind, hold-up, instinctive finishing and link play.

wc.note: Scored in 5-1 win vs Tunisia; Sweden top Group F

squad.name: Hugo Ekitiké
squad.fitNote: Mobile, direct forward who runs channels; strong alternative to Isak when fit.
squad.injury: Ruptured Achilles — long-term, rehab into start of 2026/27

squad.name: Jayden Danns
squad.fitNote: Academy striker depth.

squad.name: Cody Gakpo
squad.fitNote: Role-dependent: can work as a wide target-man, but the system's demand for pace/directness can expose him. Linked with Spurs.

squad.name: Federico Chiesa
squad.fitNote: Directness suits the winger brief but durability is the concern; LFC chasing faster wingers.

squad.name: Rio Ngumoha
squad.fitNote: Pace and dribbling fit the isolate-and-attack plan; young but exciting, especially post-Salah.

squad.name: Kaide Gordon
squad.fitNote: Talented but injury-hampered; depth.

injuries.status: Long-term — rehab expected into the start of 2026/27
Recovery being monitored ahead of pre-season
Incoming signing; expected fully fit for pre-season
Recovered — back training and playing at the World Cup

worldcup.status: Group stage — tournament began 11 Jun 2026 (USA / Canada / Mexico). 48 teams, 12 groups; group games run to 27 Jun.

target.note: Liverpool's priority winger target. Tormented Ecuador in a 1-0 win; faces Wirtz's Germany on 20 Jun.

in.meta: CB · from Rennes · age 20 (agreed Feb, joins now)

out.meta: CB · free → agreed move to Real Madrid

rumIn.meta: RW · RB Leipzig · 19 · first official bid lodged ~11 Jun

CB · Galatasaray interest; LFC won't sell
